title: Comparative: Presidential v Parliamentary
lead: India's parliamentary system buys ==executive accountability== at the cost of stability; the US presidential system buys ==stability and independence== at the risk of gridlock.

sections.title: India v USA — System by System

head: Aspect
India — Parliamentary
USA — Presidential
rows: US Constitution (1789), Articles I–III; ==strict separation of powers==.
==Single==: President is head of state AND government.
==Separate== — President and Cabinet cannot sit in Congress.
PM survives on ==LS majority== — no fixed term.
Executive orders — judicially and congressionally checked.
More flexible; fast with a majority.
